commit.message:
8354469: Keytool exposes the password in plain text when command is piped using | grep

Merge src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/util/Password.java from
jdk21u-dev f1d3049fa80260f207dc6a794485b6f582d66fe1.

Resolved conflicts in `readPassword(InputStream in, boolean isEchoOn)`:

  - Left `System.in` check as-is: `in == System.in`

Updated `Password$ConsoleHolder`:

  - Declared c2 as `Console`

  - Used `SharedSecrets.getJavaIOAccess().passwordConsole()`

  - Used `SharedSecrets.getJavaIOAccess().readPasswordNoNewLine(c2)`

Apply cleanup to sun.security.util.Password.java

Backport the
src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/util/Password.java changes
from "8291509: Minor cleanup could be done in sun.security".

8354469: Keytool exposes the password in plain text when command is piped using | grep

Backport
src/java.base/share/classes/jdk/internal/io/JdkConsoleImpl.java change
from jdk21u-dev f1d3049fa80260f207dc6a794485b6f582d66fe1 to
src/java.base/share/classes/java/io/Console.java.

8354469: Keytool exposes the password in plain text when command is piped using | grep

Backport src/java.base/share/classes/sun/security/util/Resources.java
change from jdk21u-dev f1d3049fa80260f207dc6a794485b6f582d66fe1.

test/jdk/sun/security/tools/keytool/EchoPassword.java: Fix compilation issues

With these changes the test compiles and runs on 17.  Without the fix
for 8354469 the test case fails due to passwords being echoed on the
console.

8354469: Keytool exposes the password in plain text when command is piped using | grep

Backport test changes from jdk21u-dev
f1d3049fa80260f207dc6a794485b6f582d66fe1.
